datasets.title: Office to PowerShell Reference Chain
datasets.description: A macro-lure style Office launch that transitions into encoded PowerShell, touches LSASS, and reaches an outbound host.
datasets.attackDescription: Office document launches PowerShell, then encoded execution, credential access signal, and outbound beaconing.

datasets.title: OTRF LSASS Memory Dump via COMSVCS
datasets.description: An upstream OTRF/Mordor Windows Security fixture where the host requests and reads LSASS process memory.
datasets.attackDescription: A COMSVCS-assisted LSASS dump sequence produces direct process-handle and memory-read telemetry against lsass.exe.

datasets.title: OTRF Registry Run-Key Persistence
datasets.description: An upstream OTRF/Mordor Sysmon fixture where PowerShell modifies a Windows autorun registry path.
datasets.attackDescription: PowerShell modifies an HKLM CurrentVersion Run key to establish autorun persistence for a follow-on payload.

datasets.title: OTRF PowerShell CMSTP Outbound
datasets.description: An upstream OTRF/Mordor host fixture where PowerShell associated with a CMSTP bypass sequence reaches public internet infrastructure.
datasets.attackDescription: PowerShell activity associated with a CMSTP bypass sequence reaches public IP infrastructure, producing a defensible outbound-connection signal.

datasets.title: LSASS Access Signal
datasets.description: A focused credential-access sample showing a process targetting LSASS memory.
datasets.attackDescription: A living-off-the-land binary touches LSASS memory.

datasets.title: Suspicious Outbound PowerShell
datasets.description: A PowerShell execution that opens an external connection without the full credential-access chain.
datasets.attackDescription: PowerShell stages or fetches content from an external host.

datasets.title: Administrative PowerShell Inventory
datasets.description: An intentionally ambiguous shell-to-PowerShell workflow that could be benign IT automation or a suspicious operator chain.
datasets.attackDescription: cmd.exe launches a PowerShell inventory script without payload, network, LSASS, or persistence evidence. This dataset is designed to land in needs-review territory.

replacementGuidance.attackDescription: Replace or extend the reference pack with public attack traces mapped to ATT&CK.
Upload your own lab-generated or enterprise-exported Sysmon events.
Normalize device events and alerts into the Aleens evidence schema.
